commands.review_summary:
The branch reuses the shared control-reply suppression predicate in cron delivery and expands cron delivery tests for token-only `NO_REPLY`, `ANNOUNCE_SKIP`, and `REPLY_SKIP`.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows cron delivery only normalizes `NO_REPLY`, while the shared gateway predicate already suppresses `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` and `REPLY_SKIP`; the linked issue also supplies repeated channel observations.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof or maintainer transport proof, not an automated code repair.

target.title: Suppress cron announce control replies
target.body:
Fixes #85421

Behavior addressed
- Cron announce/direct delivery now uses the shared suppressed-control-reply predicate before sending outbound payloads, so token-only `NO_REPLY`, `ANNOUNCE_SKIP`, and `REPLY_SKIP` replies are treated as silent instead of leaking to external channels.
- Existing trailing/leading `NO_REPLY` cleanup behavior is preserved.

Real environment tested
- Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `fix/cron-announce-control-reply-85421`, with the actual `dispatchCronDelivery` tests and gateway chat regression suite.

Exact steps or command run after this patch
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts
git diff --check
git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD
```

Evidence after fix
```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/andy/openclaw/openclaw-85421

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  69 passed (69)
Start at  11:02:32
Duration  2.07s (transform 1.47s, setup 243ms, import 1.52s, tests 227ms, environment 0ms)

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  29 passed (29)
Start at  11:02:31
Duration  10.14s (transform 6.81s, setup 286ms, import 2.09s, tests 7.66s, environment 0ms)

Finished in 7ms on 2 files using 1 threads.

5b478fa47d Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com> Suppress cron announce control replies
```

Observed result after fix
- Focused cron regression now exercises token-only `NO_REPLY`, `ANNOUNCE_SKIP`, and `REPLY_SKIP` through the structured/direct delivery path and verifies the outbound adapter is not called.
- Focused cron regression also exercises token-only `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` and `REPLY_SKIP` through text delivery and verifies delivery is marked attempted without sending.
- Existing gateway chat suppression behavior remains green.

What was not tested
- I did not send a real Discord/Telegram message from this branch; the regression uses the cron delivery dispatcher with mocked outbound transport to prove the payload is suppressed before provider send.

If maintainers squash or rework this PR, please preserve author attribution or include:
Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>
